name: Linzer Apple Torte
recipeIngredient: 1 cup ground hazelnuts (filberts) or almonds -- (about 5 oz.)
2 pounds apples or pears, peeled, cored, and sliced -- (6 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. CRUST: In a mixing bowl, stir together the 1 1/2 cups flour, hazelnuts, first 1/3 cup sugar and cinnamon. Cut in the 1/2 cup margarine or butter until mixture resembles small peas. Add egg, stirring to blend well. Reserve some of the melted butter for brushing later.
2. Press the remaining crust into the bottom and 1 1/4 inches up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan; set aside.
3. In a large saucepan, melt the 1/4 cup margarine or butter. Add apples and lemon juice. Cook, stirring gently, for 3-5 minutes or until apples are barely tender. Remove from heat.
4. Stir together the second 1/3 cup sugar, 2 tablespoons flour, and nutmeg in a bowl. Mix into the apple mixture.
5. Spread the apple mixture over the crust.
6. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 35-40 minutes or until the crust is brown. Cool on a rack.
7. If desired, cover and store at room temperature for up to 24 hours. Or, cover and chill for up to several days.
8. Meanwhile, form reserved crust into a ball. Roll out to an 1/8-inch thickness. Cut into 1 1/2 inch star shapes (about 10 stars total). Transfer stars to a baking sheet. Sprinkle with sugar.
9. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 5-6 minutes or until light brown; cool.
10. At serving time, remove pan sides from the torte. Spread melted preserves over top of torte. Top with baked pastry stars. Let stand until preserves set up. Makes 8-10 servings. To microwave melt preserves: place preserves in a microwave-safe dish. Cook on high for about 30 seconds or until melted.
